Regards,

Preethi

I really liked your text about task management with Cloud ALM.

However, I have a question regarding the description of user stories and requirements. I’m studying various models to use in project implementations of S/4HANA with Cloud ALM.

Could you share some models that I could use as a reference in my projects with Cloud ALM?

Best regards,

entry.title: Documents changed by the Technical User (SAP_SYSTEM)
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have some documents changed by the Technical User (SAP_SYSTEM) and I would like to understand why these documents have been changed by this user.

Thank you.

Regards,

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Documents changed by the Technical User (SAP_SYSTEM)
entry.content:
Dear All,

When a source document is released, it is expected that it will reach accounting immediately (which means, journal entry created). However, in some instances, it does not occur and the document gets stuck in Unposted Source Documents list. It could be due to a document locking issue, a period lock issue, or any other reason. Because many reasons are just temporary issues, there is a background job (known as the Cleanup Report) which runs nightly. This job tries to post all of the unposted source documents.

The job is executed by user SAP_SYSTEM. Note that the source document got finished/changed by SAP_SYSTEM user, that is a technical user with no login/user interface.

This technical user schedules a backend job, that cannot be seen on the UI, which picks all the unposted source documents for which business task does not exist and it tries to post them. Hence, in this case, you have seen document posted by SAP_SYSTEM user.

Refer to the KBA 3335891 - Document changed by the Technical User (SAP_SYSTEM) user https://me.sap.com/notes/3335891 for additional details.

Thank you.

Regards,
Anderson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: DISPATCHER GRAY
entry.content: HiC *** ERROR => Connect to database failed, rc=1, rcSQL=10 [dbhdbsql.cpp 630] C SQLCODE : 10 C SQLERRTEXT : authentication failed B ***LOG BY2=> sql error 10 performing CON [dbsh 1329]

entry.title: Re: DISPATCHER GRAY
entry.content: Hi!

entry.title: Re: DISPATCHER GRAY
entry.content: trans.log this is the messages : 4 ETW000 [ dbhdbsql.cpp,00000] *** ERROR => Connect to database failed, rc=1, rcSQL=2

entry.title: How Generative AI Helps Create Goals
entry.content:
A common and frequent problem in companies is the difficulty that employees have in setting performance and development goals. The difficulty lies, for example, in clearly defining the goal to be achieved and the milestones to be measured and how to achieve them.

Solutions that incorporate generative AI use existing content and data to create new content, including text and code generation.

SAP integrates with leading generative AI providers, such as Microsoft Azure Open AI, to create and enhance descriptive content and optimize HR activities in SAP SuccessFactors HCM.

See below how generative AI can help in setting performance and development goals.

Behavior

Performance Goal

When the employee is going to create a performance goal, in addition to the previously existing options, the employee can opt for Generative AI assistance.

 

Once you have filled in the objective in "normal language" and clicked on “Generate”, the goal will be suggested.

 

Note that in the example all fields have been filled in with suggestions.

 

 

 

After generating the information, it is possible to “regenerate” all the data at once. Fields can also be adjusted manually.

In addition to manual adjustment, the system offers AI-Assisted Writing.

The AI-assisted writing tool is an interactive tool that uses generative AI capabilities to assist employees with their writing tasks. While writing content in the SAP SuccessFactors applications, employees can leverage the features of the AI-assisted writing tool to enhance multiple aspects of their writing, such as clarity, conciseness, and tone.

The AI-assisted writing tool helps improve the content of goals, such as goal name, description, and other content provided in goal fields of text and textarea types. It isn't yet supported in goal comments of performance and development goals and custom learning activities of development goals.

 

Example of “professional” and “longer text”

 

Content generated with AI support is flagged when editing goal.

 

Development Goal

The development goal is created in a similar way to the performance goal.

When the employee is going to create a development goal, in addition to the previously existing options, the employee can opt for Generative AI assistance.

 

Suggestions are presented in the fields.

 

We have the same AI-Assisted Writing options as in the performance goals

Important information

  • For information about the languages supported by the AI-assisted writing tool, refer to SAP Note 3396784 - SuccessFactors Generative AI Multilingual Creation (1H 2024) .
  • 3391334 - SuccessFactors Generative AI Availability - SuccessFactors (1H 2024)
  • You've purchased the SAP AI Units license. For more information about SAP AI Units license, contact your Account Executive.
  • The AI ​​aims to assist the employee, to help with some activities. The responsibility for the created goal lies with the employee, who must always review all the fields suggested by the AI.
  • Goal fields available for AI generation are name, desc, metric, start, due, and milestones.
  • Custom fields of text and textarea types are additionally supported for development goals.

Note: Check the legal and regulatory framework of your organization before you enable this function. For details on how SAP SuccessFactors processes user data, please refer to SAP Sub-processors & Data Transfer Factsheets.

Further information:

AI-assisted goals 

AI-assisted writing 

First Half 2024 Release: What's new for Talent Management? We have AI-enabled features to share!

Leave a comment to any questions and I will get back to you as soon as possible!

*All images in this post are from SAP SuccessFactors Demo

entry.content:
Hi guys!

We hava a BW/4HANA 2.0 SP16 system.

And also we have an inventory ADSO with data on it.  This ADSO have 0CALDAY and 0PLANT as part of the validity table.

For testing reason I delete validity characteristic 0PLANT as you can see in the following picture

 

When I try to activate the ADSO i get the following errors

 Do I have to delete the whole content of the ADSO in order to modify the validity table or is there a way around.

Thank you,

Br. Oscar A.

entry.title: Heatwave of Innovation: A Glimpse Into the 2024 Premium Hub CoE STEM High School Summer Orientation
entry.content:
This summer, a group of bright high school students embarked on an exciting journey at SAP's week-long STEM orientation program. This program focused on harnessing the power of cloud-based AI apps using the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), providing students with practical, hands-on learning experiences using low-code/no-code tools.

 

The week kicked off with students registering for their SAP accounts and setting up trial accounts on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). A key component of the learning experience was SAP Build Apps, a powerful tool within SAP BTP that enabled students to design and develop applications using a low-code/no-code approach. This tool simplified the app creation process, allowing students to focus on functionality and creativity rather than complex coding.

The primary task was to create a barcode scanner application using SAP Build Apps. This app scanned barcodes and retrieved detailed information about food items from the Open Food Facts API, a free and open database of food product information. This integration taught students how to connect their applications to external data sources, demonstrating the potential of combining SAP BTP with other digital resources.

 

 

The real magic happened when students were introduced to Google Gemini, allowing them to enhance their applications with Generative AI capabilities. By integrating AI into their apps, students were able to analyze the health aspects of food ingredients, transforming simple barcode scanners into intelligent, interactive applications. This exercise encouraged students to think critically about how AI can enhance user experiences and provide valuable insights.

As the week progressed, students eagerly worked on their projects, each bringing their own flair to the task. They showcased the culmination of their hard work in a demo session held on Friday, presenting their enhanced applications. It was inspiring to see how each participant leveraged the tools and guidance provided to create something truly remarkable.

The orientation week was not just about building apps; it was about fostering a spirit of innovation and collaboration. The students left with more than just technical skills; they gained confidence and a deeper understanding of how technology can be used to solve real-world problems.

The feedback from participants has been overwhelmingly positive, with many expressing how the program has sparked a new interest in technology and AI. Here's what one of our students had to say about their unforgettable experience:

Participating in this program was an invaluable experience. I had the chance to meet many new people and delve deeply into SAP, low-coding program development, and generative AI. This program pushed me out of my comfort zone and provided a valuable glimpse into the future of IT. I appreciated the opportunity to grow, learn, and envision a career in this dynamic field. - Riddhi Bakde

This orientation was a testament to the power of education and technology in shaping the minds of tomorrow's innovators. As we continue to provide these opportunities, we are excited to see how these young minds will contribute to the ever-evolving tech landscape.

 

Thank you to Balaji Rao Gaddam for sponsoring and hosting this year’s event.  

Thank you to the SAP colleagues who coordinated this year’s Summer Orientation and made it a success!

Ashok Kumar M & Megan Rech (Leads) Jared Durkin (Project Co-Coordinator), Luke Korkuis (HR) and volunteers and speakers. A huge thank you to our onsite team coordinators Oswayne Smith, Nkem Ohanenye, Cecily Devaney, Nivi Rao and Jacy Yu.

And a special thanks to the SAP speakers and colleagues who shared their expertise!
John Astill (D-Shop Demo), Evan Taylor (STAR Internship Program), Justin Lynch (SAP Overview & Career Development), George Harwood (NSQ Campus Tour), Safwanur Rahman & Vivek RR (Artificial Intelligence Experts), Rakesh Gandhi and Matt Ruff (Industry 4.0 Center Tour), Manuel Gruber (MCC Presentation)

 

If you have any questions or feedback about the Summer Orientation, please contact Megan Rech.

entry.title: Dark, Rich & Smooth: My CSR Journey with Kendake Honey LTD
entry.content:
In May and June 2024, I had the pleasure of participating in the Business Women’s Network global pro-bono consulting cohort through the Acceleration Collective, SAP’s virtual pro bono consulting initiative. The cohort’s focus was empowering and amplifying women leaders and women-owned businesses in partnership with WE Connect, a global network that connects women-owned businesses to qualified buyers around the world. Together with two SAP colleagues from Netherlands and Romania, I was matched with the team of Kendake Honey LTD. And I have to say: it’s been a very rewarding experience and a great learning journey too.

Kendake Honey is a social enterprise based in UK. Chibugo Okafor started her business with the social objective of utilizing the production from 400 beehives, processing and packaging an average of 5.7 tonnes of high-quality organic African honey annually. With this, Chi and team can empower and provide job opportunities to (to date over) 1.200 female and youth beekeepers to become sustainable income generators. Today, 70% of farmers are female, yet women operate on a smaller scale resulting in lower yields than those operated by men. This is largely contributed to the fact that women have access to few inputs and resources such as fertilizers, trainings on correct harvesting techniques or credit. If women are given equal access to resources as men, national productivity in developing countries can rise by 2.5 – 4%, lifting as many as 100 – 150 million people out of chronic hunger. The numbers speak for themselves, simply mind-blowing. 

During the CSR assignment, we focused on how to expand Kendake Honey’s B2B range as well as increase the B2C reach. We worked through various areas and laid out suitable next steps. Was it easy? No. Beside the short time frame to become one team, such engagement requires to step outside of the comfort zone and dive into new business areas and market conditions. You need to approach the task with an open mind, and no bold assumptions should be made on how the enterprise operates. Take time to first understand their products and goals as well as challenges. Trust, they know their business better than you. Then jointly explore solutions, break them down into short- to mid-term steps under consideration of team capabilities and financial means. The experience also reminded me of how important it is to seek an outside-in view. We tend to dive too deep into topics and can easily overlook simple yet efficient and effective steps. Asking for an external opinion or advise can help to energize the process and make us even better. If you have not yet done so, keep learning and find yourself a great mentor, advisor or simply someone who you look up to.
To sum it up: by taking on the CSR assignment, you are committing to invest extra time and expertise to support a social enterprise. Yet, all the valuable takeaways and learnings are truly rewarding and make this journey worth all the efforts.    

I would recommend this experience to everyone who is willing to help others succeed and explore beyond their own areas of expertise. It was my honor to support Kendake Honey’s team and their mission. A profitable and sustainable business producing an organic honey with highest certification standards, delivering health benefits and amazing taste (firsthand experience 😊). Running protective measures for the beehives, without a doubt of a high importance for our planet. And: advocating for and empowering African women to secure their own income and become independent female farmers. Kendake Honey’s founder and team are an inspiration of how to make a positive change in the world: embrace your cultural heritage, connect the dots, break stereotypes or outdated society expectations, and never stop believing you can do it. I am more than grateful to have had the opportunity being part of Kendake Honey – a women-owned and women-powered social enterprise.

I’d like to close off my reflections with a quote from Chi’s mom: “If you are going to do it at all, give it your all” – for me, the magic essence for every success story.

To find out more: TED Talk by Chibugo Okafor and visit Kendake Honey’s website

entry.title: Echoes of Excellence: Highlights from the Disability:IN Conference & Expo
entry.content:
As SAP’s Autism at Work program marks its 11th year, its impact continues to resonate across industries, inspiring numerous companies to embrace the journey toward autism inclusivity. Through strategic partnerships and committed advocacy, the program has established itself as a leader in promoting a mor inclusive workplace for individuals on the autism spectrum. 

 For the second consecutive year, SAP partnered with Disability:IN to recognize and honor organizations that are making significant strides in promoting autism inclusion. This collaboration led to the creation of the Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award, which was presented at the Disability:IN Conference & Expo in Las Vegas, Nevada on July 16, 2024.   The Autism Inclusion Company of the Year Award was presented to DXC at the Disability:IN Conference. SAP Autism at Work program played a pivotal role in advising on the award’s selection criteria, while Disability:IN independently reviewed the submissions and selected the winner, ensuring a fair and impactful recognition process. 

SAP’s commitment to inclusivity was further underscored when it was named a Best Place to Work for People with Disabilities in Brazil, the United States, Canada, and Germany in 2024. This accolade reflects our ongoing efforts to expand accessibility for employees and partners around the world, and our accessibility-focused Employee Network Groups are valuable contributors to such practices and culture across SAP. The Disability Equality Index, which evaluates companies across six categories—Culture & Leadership, Enterprise-Wide Access, Employment Practices, Community Engagement, and Supplier Diversity—highlighted SAP's excellence, marking the sixth consecutive year that SAP America achieved a perfect score of 100. 

SAP’s dedication to inclusivity was also evident at the 2024 Global Disability: IN Conference, which offered a platform for sharing insights and best practices on disability inclusion. With over 5,000 attendees participating both in-person and virtually, the conference featured sessions on crucial topics such as self-identification, accessibility, creating inclusive content, AI integration, talent acquisition, and supporting neurodivergent employees.  

 

After a successful event, we gathered some exciting experiences and meaningful reflections from our peers and colleagues.  

Our colleagues, Liliana (Lili) Zolt (she/they), Head of Influencer Strategy for SAP SuccessFactors, Haldis Gonsalves (She/Her), Senior Director, Platform AI Experience & Strategy, and Ezequiel Massa (he/him), Head of Diversity & Inclusion, SAP Latin America & Caribbean, provided compelling testimonies on the profound lessons learned from the conference and offered valuable insights into the key takeaways. To fully appreciate their perspectives, it’s essential to understand their backgrounds. Haldis, Ezequiel, and Liliana’s personal experiences play a crucial role in illustrating how inclusivity can be seamlessly woven into every individual’s journey. By drawing on different perspectives, we can find how lived experiences can be instrumental in fostering a deeper, more authentic approach to inclusivity and inclusive best practices.  

 Let’s dive into Liliana’s story: 

Liliana is the Global Co-Lead for SAP HEAR & Friends: SAP’s employee network group (ENG) for people with hearing loss and their allies. Profoundly Deaf and fluent in both English and American Sign Language (ASL), Lili is a cochlear implant recipient committed to fostering a tight-knit Deaf community within and outside of SAP.  

Lili was one of the attendees of the Disability:IN Global Conference in Las Vegas, Nevada. Over three busy days filled with networking, learning, and fun, Liliana identified key takeaways from the conference: trends within AI, storytelling, and accessibility. “For starters, AI will accelerate inclusivity and create more opportunities for disabled talent in the future of work. Secondly, focusing on intersectional representation in storytelling empowers individuals and strengthens brand relationships. Lastly, organizations that prioritize accessibility from the get-go are ahead of the game, and the future requires organizations to move past individual-use cases and ensure longevity of their brand by setting the standard as access for all”, shared Lili.     

Now, let’s learn more about Ezequiel’s story: 

Ezequiel has been working in the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) space for the past 10 years, considering the time invested in volunteer assignments where he was able to turn his purpose and calling into his full-time role as Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion at SAP LAC in 2020. Through his foundation as a results-oriented leader in the tech industry, he is skilled in leading by influence, public speaking, and community engagement.  In 2019, Ezequiel was selected by OUTstanding and Yahoo Finance as one of the “50 LGBT+ Future Leaders”. 

While reflecting on his own experience at the conference, Ezequiel shared, “Attending the Disability:IN Conference is always a powerful learning experience, yet it's not just about the lessons learned if you ask me. What I treasure most of these opportunities is having the chance to connect with fellow SAP colleagues supporting disability inclusion around the globe and hearing about the impressive work other organizations are driving. Being Co-Chair to the Disability:IN LATAM Council for the past three years and in my capacity as Head of D&I for Latin America at SAP, the main highlight for me was seeing SAP Brazil score 100 points on the Disability Equality Index. I'm feeling proud and energized to strengthen our work in the region together with our leaders and Employee Network Groups”. 

 Lastly, let’s look at Haldis’ story:  

Haldis Gonsalves, Senior Director at SAP in Platform AI Experience & Strategy, combines her personal experiences with ASD and ADHD with her professional expertise to champion neurodiversity in the workplace. Since joining SAP in 2020, she has worked on multiple cross-product initiatives, including SAP Unified Analytics, SAP Task Center, SAP Unified Services and BTP Experience Platform Vision and Strategy. Haldis is dedicated to creating a more inclusive tech industry, using AI to improve accessibility and support diverse communities.   

After her time at the event, Haldis shared, "Attending my first Disability:IN Conference this year was both eye-opening and deeply personal. It was validating to see neurodiversity at the forefront of discussions and to connect my AI work with practical applications driving inclusivity. One standout moment was exploring how multimodal GenAI assistants can improve the daily lives of neurodivergent employees, including myself, and empower ERGs to create more inclusive workplaces. A key takeaway that resonated with me was the need to expand job opportunities beyond entry-level roles for neurodivergent talent. This really hit home because it's crucial to show that inclusivity extends beyond just getting in the door—it’s about creating pathways for growth and career advancement, especially for those of us diagnosed later in life. Being part of a conference that truly celebrated and pushed for these changes was inspiring and empowering."

 

The success of the conference and the recognition of SAP as a leader in disability inclusion reflect the company's unwavering commitment to creating a more inclusive workplace for all. As we continue to advance these efforts, we invite you to take an active step towards autism inclusion by signing the Autism Inclusion Pledge. Together, we can build a future where everyone can thrive.

entry.title: Re: DISPATCHER GRAY
entry.content: i do the note 2961140 and this is the result: to level database hd4adm@testhanadb:/usr/sap/HD4/HDB00/exe> hdbuserstore list DATA FILE : /usr/sap/HD4/home/.hdb/testhanadb/SSFS_HDB.DAT KEY FILE : /usr/sap/HD4/home/.hdb/testhanadb/SSFS_HDB.KEY KEY HD4SAPDBCTRL ENV : testhanadb:30013 USER: SAPDBCTRL KEY MYSYSTEM ENV : testhanadb:30013 USER: SYSTEM KEY DEFAULT ENV : testhanadb:30003 USER: SYSTEM ACTIVE RECORDS : 10 DELETED RECORDS : 9 NUMBER OF COMPLETE KEY: 3 Operation succeed. to level OS testhanaapp:s4dadm 1169> hdbuserstore list DATA FILE : /home/s4dadm/.hdb/testhanaapp/SSFS_HDB.DAT KEY FILE : /home/s4dadm/.hdb/testhanaapp/SSFS_HDB.KEY KEY DEFAULT ENV : testhanadb:30013 USER: SAPHANADB ACTIVE RECORDS : 4 DELETED RECORDS : 31 NUMBER OF COMPLETE KEY: 1 Operation succeed.

entry.title: Re: Task 1 - Create CDS View Entity ( August Developer Challenge )

entry.title: SAP PPDS
entry.content:
I have a strange case in my PPDS. I have one material with Fc req/ PIRs in RRP3 view and

Those PIRs are not their in MD04.

I want to delete those PIRs in RRP3 but it is not happening. I tried using /sapapo/ md74 and MD74. 

 

Please help me out and there are no planned orders for those PIRs

entry.title: Re: How can i upgrade SAP Gui 8.0 from 7.5 with command lines.
entry.content:
Hello,

Are you using SAP GUI 800 32bit or 64bit? You can verify that each has a different name for the command line in SAP Setup.

To make an upgrade you need first to uninstall the previous product version via command line:

NwSapSetup.exe /Silent /Uninstall /Product="<product_name>"

Then to install the new product:

C:\Users\<user>\Installation Server\Setup\NwSapSetup.exe /Silent /Product="<product_name>"

<user> and <product_name> are placeholders which content may vary depending on the user executing the command and the target product.

Also /silent parameter is optional but it causes the process to be executed silently on background, it can be replaced by /nodlg (no dialog but with visible progress bar) or you can completely remove this parameter if wanted.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: FreeHand SQL query with input parameters
entry.content:
Hi @jlopezm,

That's a good question! I'm inclined to think that parameters are not supported in SAC for SAP Datasphere OData connections. Although it seems that Freehand Query is a solution for CDS views with parameters on OData connections. Refer to 2936924 - Input Parameter on CDS Views does not work via Query Panel from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

Have you tried? 

CV_MOV_CCEE_RD_WP(P_MANDT='100',P_DATE_FROM=1980-01-01,P_DATE_TO=1980-01-01)/Results

Please note that parameter values are case sensitive, if I'm not mistaken.

Thanks,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: Kyma Timeout LoadBalancer Postgres

entry.title: Re: Can not use replication flows to replicate CDS Views...
entry.content:
Hi @sophie2024,

There might be something going on in the S/4HANA systems, maybe some SAP Notes need to be applied, etc. You can check the S/4HANA backend and see if there are any clues for the error you are getting. Second, have you tried the same CDS view with a data flow?

Thanks,
Tuncay

entry.title: How to change draw signature dimensions and borders
entry.content:
Hello,
in Build Apps (free tier) I would like to change the style of the "Draw signature" box. Both Android tablet and smartphone render it quite small and the users are complaining that it's hard to use, also because there is no border to show it better.
I am looking for a way to style the width, the height and the border (or at least the background colour).
Do you know how to do it?
Many thanks

entry.title: SAC – data model updating a date dimension from a Timestamp to Date
entry.content:
Hello SAC Experts,

When I create a data model in SAC, a date dimensions are setup as a timestamp type, when I update the date such as EFFECTIVE_DATE to a date type,

 

It changes it to a dimension table.

Has anyone encountered this? What did you do to get the date to actual update to a date type?

entry.title: Re: Agentry CT drop down doesn't populate record if there is only one matching record with cascade i
entry.content: Sudhir - Have you used the Test Environment to debug your cascade rule Z_ReferenceEquipmentParent? Can you see that it is returning the expected value when you have multiple matches and singles?

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

I am keenly interested in participating in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative. My academic background includes a degree in Mechanical Engineering, and I am currently pursuing an MBA with a specialization in Operations Management. Professionally, I have interned in supply chain and lean manufacturing roles, where I developed a strong foundation in operations, sourcing, and logistics.

I believe I am a great fit for this program as I belong to three of the eligible categories:

- I am from Nagpur, a small city in India, with limited access to economic opportunities compared to larger urban areas.
- I belong to the Scheduled Caste Buddhist community, which faces unique challenges in accessing economic and educational resources.
- I am a first-generation college student striving to create opportunities for myself and others in my community.

I am excited to enhance my skills through SAP Certification, and I look forward to learning and contributing within the SAP ecosystem. I am also ready to engage in the beginner corner for guidance and support throughout this journey.

Thank you for considering my application.

Best regards,

Yash

entry.title: Re: Set up real-time trigger-based replication from MSSQL to SAP HANA Cloud (or on-prem) using SAP S
entry.content:
Hello Community,

We have a requirement to replicate data from S/4 to Convergent Mediation HANA DB and were recommended to use SDI with ABAP Adapter. Does ABAP adapter support real time replication from Source to Target? How can we ensure both initial load and real time replication in our use case.

entry.title: Re: Inconsistent Replication Flow Behavior in SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Re: How to create an SAP Datasphere service instance in the SAP BTP Platform?
entry.content:
@dwag_graffseb - Yes, you can create a new subaccount space within the same subaccount and create a free SAP Datasphere instance.

Thank You,

Jeet

entry.title: Re: Create Standard Business Partner from Custom RAP Appl...
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @rajib3301.

I got multiple resources for you: 

  • Check out the API Business Accelerator Hub at api.sap.com. Click SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, then APIs, then OData V2, then in the Search field enter "Business Partner" and select "Business Partner (A2X)"   
  • Definitely check out this guy's video Exploring API specifications on the SAP API Business Hub, especially about one hour in: https://www.youtube.com/live/tW0-96gGQAs?si=XG9YT2_fRqHkoXUv&t=3300.
  • Devtober Fest video 🟢 A Beginner's Guide to the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (youtube.com)
  • Your intended outcome appears to point to a Node Extension, but please explore the RAP model more thoroughly: Node Extensions | SAP Help Portal

     Good luck! 

If this helped address your request, please mark this response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to check average number of users connecting to th...
entry.content:
You can get the number of users starting all SAP Fiori applications with the Fiori App Usage Report from the Fiori Tracker Suite.

You can get Fiori Tracker free by letting us feature your company name.

Regards
Greg Malewski

entry.title: Re: Unlocking SAP BTP: Your Guide to SAP Business Technology Platform Use Case One-Pagers
entry.content:
Hi @joanneks,

Thank you for posting the blog. I'd love to try out the One-Pager. However, I don't have access to the link. Is it publicly available yet?

entry.title: Re: J1INJV
entry.content:
First I want to answer your question

  1. Why the system says the document is not define.
  •     Well, the answer is because part of the configuration for this report is the assignment of a document type to the report. So this means either there is no document define for this report OR you are using a document type which was not define for the report. So either find a document which was define for the functionality OR have your configuration team to define one.

Now for your question  Why we use J1INJV?

  • Well.. This is to create another FI document called Voucher. This document will adjust the Tax amounts and percentages as required. This document should not be reverse by regular transactions like FB03, but should be reverse by this J1INJV transaction.
  • This report has 2 options to be run  1) with Reference to another Vendor document 2) Without Reference to another vendor Invoice

 

  1. Option With Reference
  • If you use the option of with reference, you enter the vendor invoice number to be corrected and the system will show the document line items and will open all the tax line amount fields for you to change. Keep in mind this option ONLY opens the amount field, so you will not be able to change the tax levels or tax code. You will be able only to change amounts. 
  • If you use the reference Option, you don't have to enter the document number and the business place. This will open another screen where you can enter a new Tax code and all the fields in your tax procedure. This will allow you to enter amount in your tax different levels (i.e. GST, SGST, IGST). So this option will actually reclass tax codes and tax accounts. But will not make reference to particular vendor invoice

I hope this helps you.

entry.title: Re: Widget color is not displayed when SAC story is execu...
entry.content: @William_Yu : Expecting the same as issue might be related to lighter view. Case is already been opened with SAP and still awaiting for the inputs .

entry.title: SAP Business One 10.0 FP2305 - Error activating appliance
entry.content:
After 5 hours waiting for the activation to finish, I decided to check the progress of the process on the linux server. I noticed that the installation was stuck on an error message, the installation of the "net-tools" package was missing. I understand that the installation script is not up to date according to the latest versions of SUSE and Business One.


How to trigger SAP to make this correction?

entry.title: Re: excel opening automatically while running report
entry.content: Hi Junaidshaik284, did you get the solution for this, or did you replace the FM?

entry.title: Re: How to maintain multiple Payment Methods in Business...
entry.content:
Thank you for your request, @FredLacaze.

In the SAP Help you can find a comparison of Business Partner applications: Feature Comparison for Business Partner Master Data Apps | SAP Help Portal. In my understanding, these apps are not mutually exclusive. You may assign authorization for either to a user, where that makes business sense. 

Further down in the documentation, under Concepts and Functions, you can find 

  • Business Partner Bank Account Data | SAP Help Portal, and 
  • Payment Cards | SAP Help Portal.   

If that addressed the request, please mark the response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: ECCS periodic rollup file does not pick up the partner unit in the upload file
entry.content:
We are on SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 and we are using SAP EC-CS with the periodic rollup functionality to send SAP FI data to EC-CS. The file does not pick up for one fs item (that is assigned to one gl account) the partner information and the fs item has a breakdown that allows to post a partner unit for the fs item.

In the table GLT3 the gl account has a partner unit but in the periodic file does not have any.

Is there any configuration setting for SAP EC-CS periodic role up file where we could configure this?

entry.title: Re: No sale pages en el home de S4hana cloud public edition
entry.content:
I machine-translated your post to English like this, @AdrianSoto: 

If you don't see them in pages in S/4hana cloud public edition, the solution is to enable the interests, so that they appear in the home.
 

Thank you for providing the answer to the question right away. For future reference, you may want to phrase out the problem statement in the body of the post. Once posted, use the "Answer" function to respond to your own post with your solution. Once you posted the solution accept the response as solution. This way, both your question and the solution may be picked up by the algorithm and your answer may be suggested to fellow community members who might search for respective content or start typing a similar question.
Thank you.

entry.title: Show images in SmartForms
entry.content:
Hello everyone!
How can I show images in the content or main of a smartform, other than the logo.
Considering the following options that I have tried:

1. Saving the images in a network folder and in a Z table saving the path of that image.
2. Save the images in transaction SE78 and save the image name in a Z table.

Later, read that saved image in the smartform.

I'm new to creating smartforms, I have tried these 2 options but none of them work for me, could someone guide me if it is possible to do it this way or is there another way to do it?

Greetings

entry.title: Re: g/l account field enquiry
entry.content:
Thank you for your question, @Connie__.

The API for G/L Accounts in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub shows, there is a field 

GLAccountExternal

available, which could be used for their purposes. It would otherwise be used as a reference field in master data migration, when accounts get renumbered in S/4HANA Cloud.

If this satisfies your request, please mark the response accordingly. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Digital Manufacturing | Design Production Processes Get Active Shift

Hi gurus,

Do you know if it is possible to know in a design production process app whether or not there is an active shift at the current time?

If it is possible, could you guide me through the steps to follow in a design production process app on how I could do this?

I thank you in advance for your help.
Kind regards,

SAP Digital Manufacturing

entry.content:
Hi guys,

I am trying to create a subscription within the manage automatic triggers app, but the system gives me the following error:

 

I have the subscription configured as follows:

 

The PPD that I want to run when an indicator changes value is configured as follows:

 

 

The same PPD works correctly if I configure it as a Timer, but if I configure it as a subscription it gives me the error.

Do you have any idea what the problem could be?

Thank you very much for the help.
Kind regards,

SAP Digital Manufacturing  SAP Digital Manufacturing Insights

entry.title: FM READ_TEXT is not showing the data but the SAP standard transaction ME33L does show all the data
entry.content:
hello,

I am using FM READ_TEXT to read some texts (  EKPO, L03 ) but the FM is not showing any data. When I check the database table STXH also the DB table is not showing any data, but however, when I go to the SAP standard transaction ME33L ,  SAP  uses the same FM READ_TEXT  and the FM does show all the data !

If I debug the FM READ_TEXT  this is exactly the problem

1)  In my program when I use the FM  READ_TEXT

     syst-subrc = 4   (  not found )

2) In SAP program ,  when SAP uses the FM READ_TEXT

     syst-subrc = 0   (  everything ok !  )

 

any suggestions guys ?

thanks in advance,

Jimmy

entry.title: Re: SAP extends ERP cloud portfolio with "SAP ERP, private edition, option for Microsoft SQL&qu

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Onboarding Resources
entry.content: How to enter the test environment practice?

entry.title: Re: Installing Composable Storefront for SAP Commerce 2211.20
entry.content:
Hi @kmanur ,

If you make use of yarn rather than npm to Start your server.

npm install -g yarn: Install Yearn 

yarn install: Install Dependencies for a project using yarn

yarn start --ssl : This will open HTTPS port for accessing a storefront.

 

Hope this helps

Regards

Rohit

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne September '24
entry.content:
Melbourne! we are back again for the 4th time this year. And maybe there is a spring in your step as we head out of winter...

Last meet up was fantastic with a great mix of fresh faces and those familiar regulars. And as always really interesting conversations.

By now you know the drill, and if not check out the previous Stammtische from this year (March, May & June). Essentially, just come along for as long as you like if you can. No need to book or register, just join the fun. The venue has a relaxed vibe, excellent for conversation over a drink and good food (all optional). Reach out if you have any questions though.

The November Melbourne Stammtisch is slated to coincide with the TechEd on Tour event - details to come...

Looking forward to catching you there, and please remember to spread the word around.

Thursday 12th September 2024, 5:30pm onwards at The Bank on Collins 

 Ground floor/394 Collins St, Melbourne VIC 3000.

 

When you arrive, if you can't find us you can ask for a booking for the "SAP Regulars Table"

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA federated roles assignment to users in BTP via SAP Identity Provisioning Service
entry.content:
Thanks Amalakar for the Informative blog. With this approach, can I confirm the following? 

1. Every role from s/4 HANA must have a corresponding user group in IAS and this user group needs to be created manually.

2. Every user group in IAS must be manually assigned to Role collection in BTP. 

If above understanding is correct, Is it possible to eliminate the manual creation of user groups and assigment to role collection?

entry.title: Encryption and decryption
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created below sample program for encrypt and decrypt. It works fine. However, my requirement is to create two separate program modules one for encrypt and the other decrypt. The decrypt value will be passed from SAP service gateway. Once I separated into two modules. It fails to decrypt. Why?

REPORT ztest1.
*REPORT zged_aes.

DATA lv_message_string TYPE string.
DATA: lv_in_string TYPE string VALUE '20241001|T520|30000001|0018014695|20240903174922'.

WRITE:/ lv_in_string.

" create message
DATA(lr_conv_sec) = cl_abap_conv_out_ce=>create( ).
lr_conv_sec->write( data = lv_in_string ).

" create key
DATA(lr_conv_key) = cl_abap_conv_out_ce=>create( ).
lr_conv_key->write( data = 'MySymmetricKey' ).

" encrypt using AES256
cl_sec_sxml_writer=>encrypt(
  EXPORTING
    plaintext =  lr_conv_sec->get_buffer( )
    key =        lr_conv_key->get_buffer( )
    algorithm =  cl_sec_sxml_writer=>co_aes256_algorithm
  IMPORTING
    ciphertext = DATA(lv_message) ).


WRITE lv_message.

DATA: lv_message1 TYPE xstring.

*lv_message1 = lv_message.


DATA: ls_bin  TYPE c LENGTH 255,
      lv_itab LIKE TABLE OF ls_bin.

DATA len TYPE i.
*
CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_XSTRING_TO_BINARY'
  EXPORTING
    buffer        = lv_message
*   APPEND_TO_TABLE       = ' '
  IMPORTING
    output_length = len
  TABLES
    binary_tab    = lv_itab.

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_BINARY_TO_STRING'
  EXPORTING
    input_length = len
*   FIRST_LINE   = 0
*   LAST_LINE    = 0
*   MIMETYPE     = ' '
*   ENCODING     =
  IMPORTING
    text_buffer  = lv_message_string
*   OUTPUT_LENGTH       =
  TABLES
    binary_tab   = lv_itab
  EXCEPTIONS
    failed       = 1
    OTHERS       = 2.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.

WRITE:/ lv_message_string.

CALL FUNCTION 'SCMS_STRING_TO_XSTRING'
  EXPORTING
    text   = lv_message_string
*   mimetype = space
*   encoding =
  IMPORTING
    buffer = lv_message1
*  EXCEPTIONS
*   failed = 1
*   others = 2
  .
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*   WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

WRITE:/ lv_message1.

" decrypt message

cl_sec_sxml_writer=>decrypt(
  EXPORTING
    ciphertext = lv_message
    key =        lr_conv_key->get_buffer( )
    algorithm =  cl_sec_sxml_writer=>co_aes256_algorithm "_pem
  IMPORTING
    plaintext =  DATA(lv_message_decrypted) ).

" convert xstring to string for output
cl_abap_conv_in_ce=>create( input = lv_message_decrypted )->read( IMPORTING data = lv_message_string ).
*
*" output secret message
WRITE lv_message_string.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDC won't send admin TFA Reset Email to the person who triggered it and not the admin
entry.content: According to the documentation, the link is supposed to "send the administrator a TFA reset email. Once the user clicks the link, they are able to reset their TFA device." https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_DATA_CLOUD/8b8d6fffe113457094a17701f63e3d6a/1112b9c80af34393a7895fc0133212e7.html?q=send%20tfa

entry.title: Re: SAP CDC won't send admin TFA Reset Email to the perso...
entry.content:
According to the documentation, the link is supposed to "send the administrator a TFA reset email. Once the user clicks the link, they are able to reset their TFA device." Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_DATA_CLOUD/8b8d6fffe113457094a17701f63e3d6a/1112b9c80af34393a7895fc0133212e7.html?q=send%20tfa

If you can, please open a ticket within the SAP support portal (https://me.sap.com/home) and report the issue. Note: You'll need an S-User ID to log in. Support should also be able to trigger a TFA reset from their end.

entry.title: Re: Signavio Process Insights: insufficient data collecti...
entry.content:
Hi Rounak,

Based on what you have written, it appears that there isn't enough data in the source system (for the reference period) that is being access by Process Insights.

Please be aware that each performance indicator in Process Insights has its own predefined reference period for data extraction. This is stored in the source system. Please see the Process Insights Help Document link to understand this in more details. - link

You can adjust the reference period to increase the data flow into Process Insight.

This SAP note (3236298) talks about reducing the reference period to avoid memory dumps during data extraction. You can do the opposite of this, to allow more data to flow into Process Insights.

Hope this helps. If this doesn't, feel free to reach out to the relevant Signavio Partner Alliance team or Signavio Support and we can get you the relevant support.

Cheers,
Projit

entry.title: Reduce WM stock without affecting FI documents
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement to remove the SLOC-WM configuration, Whereas the stock is available in the WM level under the sloc. 

Question : How can we reduce the stock at WM level only without creating the FI documents?

Additional question: Can we transfer the stock from one batch to other batch in the WM level only?

Note: No FI postings for above all Queries.

Thanks,
Saikumar

entry.title: Re: SAC – data model updating a date dimension from a Tim...
entry.content:
Hi AS5, 

    Dimension without dimension table can only be type Text, Timestamp or Generic(when create model from data).  I assume it shouldn't be a problem for dimension like 'Effctive_Date' to have dimension table. 

Best regards, William

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Migration: Insights and Guidance for IT Leaders
entry.content: Thanks for bringing this to community's attention. Sadly, the guide appears to be outdated on publication. I am referring to the page 89. This is the 2nd page of "Manage your RICEFW". I could note several references to "old products" in the column "New technology". It would be great if SAP could update its latest document with its own latest brand names (ex RPA) and remove outdated products (ex CPI-DS) from this list. This would save the rest of us several days effort in discussions with the target audience of this document.

entry.title: Ledger 0L data Are there any duplicates for the example 3 in the note 2554992 ?
entry.content:
In the example 3 of the NOTE 2554992 complaint, the 0L2L Ledger group contains both 0L and 2L ledgers, and depreciation areas 01 and 15 are posted to the 0L2L Ledger group, so that depreciation areas 01 and 15 are posted to both the 0L ledger and the 2L ledger. Will this result in documents relating to asset postings (e.g. asset depreciation) in either the 0L or 2L ledger data containing data for both depreciation areas, i.e. will this result in duplicate data? How can this be avoided? 

However, I think in this scenario, the ledger 0L would only need to post data in the 01 depreciation area; and 2L would only need to post data in the 15 depreciation area.

Thanks and regards!

entry.title: HI. Thanks in advance and Can any one explain how many ledgers we can assign to company code
entry.content:
Hi 

How many ledger we can assign to company code including leading and non ledgers.

My understanding we can assign three accounting principles including local gaap. That means we can assign each accounting principle to each ledger and it comes to max 3 ledgers.

Pl correct me if I am wrong

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Remove and add zeroes in S4 HANA
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, the use of function modules like CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_INPUT and CONVERSION_EXIT_ALPHA_OUTPUT is restricted in some cases, especially when working in a restricted or cloud-based environment. Since you're getting the error "Not permitted in restricted scope," it means you're not allowed to use these function modules in your context.

To handle the removal and addition of leading zeros without using these function modules, you can manually manipulate the strings in ABAP code. 

Here's an alternative approach:

1. Remove Leading Zeros

You can use the SHIFT statement to remove leading zeros from a string.

DATA(lv_field) = '000123456'. " Example value with leading zeros
SHIFT lv_field LEFT DELETING LEADING '0'.
WRITE: lv_field. " Output: 123456

2. Add Leading Zeros Based on Data Type Length

To add leading zeros back, you can use the CONDENSE and FORMAT options, or pad it using the WRITE statement.

DATA: lv_output TYPE string,
lv_length TYPE i.

lv_output = '123456'. " Example value after removing zeros
lv_length = 10. " Desired length with leading zeros

" Add leading zeros to match the required length
lv_output = lv_output+0(lv_length).
WRITE lv_output LEFT-JUSTIFIED TO lv_output.

WRITE: lv_output. " Output: 0000123456 if length is 10

Explanation:

- SHIFT: Removes leading zeros from the string.
- lv_length: Represents the target length of the field after adding zeros.
- Padding: The lv_output = lv_output+0(lv_length) ensures that the string is padded with leading zeros to match the desired length.

entry.title: Re: Deleting a Table from Excel Using ABAP2XLSX
entry.content:
To delete a table from an Excel file using the ABAP2XLSX library, you can follow these steps:

1. Identify the Table: First, you need to identify the table you want to delete within the worksheet. You should have a reference to the worksheet where the table resides.

2. Remove the Table: While there isn't a direct method to delete a table in the ZCL_EXCEL_WORKSHEET class, you can remove the rows or the entire content associated with that table.

3. Example Code: Here is a sample code snippet that demonstrates how to clear the contents of a worksheet, effectively removing the table data:

DATA: lo_excel TYPE REF TO zcl_excel,
lo_worksheet TYPE REF TO zcl_excel_worksheet.

" Create an instance of the Excel class
CREATE OBJECT lo_excel.

" Load the existing Excel file
lo_excel->load( 'path_to_your_file.xlsx' ).

" Get the worksheet where the table is located
lo_worksheet = lo_excel->get_worksheet( 'Sheet1' ). " Change 'Sheet1' to your sheet name

" Clear the content of the worksheet (which includes tables)
lo_worksheet->clear( ).

" Save the changes to the Excel file
lo_excel->save( 'path_to_your_file.xlsx' ).

4. Considerations:

The clear method will remove all data from the worksheet, so use it carefully. If you only want to remove specific rows or columns, you will need to implement additional logic to target just those.
If your goal is to remove only the formatting or styles related to the table, you may need to explore methods for modifying cell styles instead.

5. Consult Documentation: If you require more specific functionality, it's always good to refer to the ABAP2XLSX documentation or check the source code of the classes to see if there are other available methods that fit your needs.

entry.title: Re: Can not use replication flows to replicate CDS Views...
entry.content:
Check ABAP: RODPS_REPL_TEST. Will provide you more clues. Your CDS view may be too complex for the automated CDC to use the primary key. Try own delta 

Annotation: 

delta.byElement : {
name: '[FIELD]',

entry.title: Re: Inconsistent Replication Flow Behavior in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi, Samuel

delta? 

the replication flow is not hanging. It is in listening mode. This is how the replication flow behaves when waiting for delta. 

Martin

Hope you are doing well!!!!

I'm Soumik Dey from India. I am working as a Management Consultant for the past 7 years. My domain is project management & anything realted to PmaaS(Project Management as a Service). Firstly, you are doing a very noble thing in providing thousands of professional to upskill their career at zero cost. That is quite a big thing! Speaking about me - I'm looking to upskill myself by learning several SAP modules that will exceptionally boost my career. Some of the modules that I am really interested to complete are:

- SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management

- SAP Project Systems

- SAP HANA

It is my humble & earnest request for a chance at doing free certifications that would really help me elevate my career & enter into the SAP domain with vigour & fulfill my goal to transition my career

 

Thanks & Regards

Soumik Dey

entry.title: Re: Encryption and decryption
entry.content: Please use the buttons "..." and "</>" to display your code nicely. See how to do it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-code-in-sap-community-gt-2024/qaq-p/13684774

entry.title: Re: FM READ_TEXT is not showing the data but the SAP standard transaction ME33L does show all the da
entry.content: Debug READ_TEXT when it's called from ME33L, copy ALL of the EXACT values of the parameters into your custom report with CTRL+C and CTRL+V, and it should work. Make sure to run your report from the same client than the one where you run ME33L.

entry.title: Re: Show images in SmartForms
entry.content: Note that SE78/Smart Form images SUPPORT ONLY BMP 24-bit colors (if not you must also convert the image). "Save the images in transaction SE78" PROGRAMATICALLY -> see the blog post (skip the QR code part) https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/how-to-create-a-qr-code-and-show-it-in-a-smartform/ba-p/13253258

entry.title: Re: excel opening automatically while running report
entry.content: @Octavio_SAjust search "abap2xlsx"

entry.title: Seeking Guidance on Choosing the Right Learning Path for SAP Digital Skills Initiative
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I’m excited to be part of the SAP Digital Skills Initiative and eager to begin my learning journey. With over 25 years of experience in custom development using Oracle tools, including hands-on expertise in technologies like .NET, Oracle APEX, ABAP, and databases, I’ve recently shifted my focus towards SAP HANA and the broader SAP ecosystem.

As a General Manager in IT with an MBA in Finance, I have led technical teams and worked extensively on SAP modules such as HCM, PP, MM, and administration using Basis and ABAP with Crystal Reports. Given my experience with enterprise systems and my current interest in transitioning more deeply into SAP HANA, I’m looking for advice on which learning path to pursue.

Would it be more beneficial for me to focus on SAP HANA administration and development, or should I explore other areas like SAP S/4HANA or specific functional modules such as Finance or Supply Chain? I’d greatly appreciate any guidance from experts on the best direction for someone with my background and interests to make the most impact in the SAP landscape.
